title: Neurotropis kotschyana
taxonomy: Neurotropis kotschyana is a species in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Neurotropis, which is a group of flowering plants. Neurotropis kotschyana is native to the Middle East and parts of Asia, specifically in Turkey, Iran, and Iraq. The classification of Neurotropis kotschyana is further detailed at the level of tribe (Lithospermeae) and order (Boraginales). Within the Boraginaceae family, Neurotropis kotschyana shares characteristics with other members of the genus.

intro: Neurotropis kotschyana, also known as Kotschy's neurotropis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health conditions due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce pain, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Neurotropis kotschyana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neurotropis kotschyana is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as dyspepsia, flatulence, and gastrointestinal disorders.
